contribution in Section 4 is to propose a novel framework for coun-

concepts.finding:
Most existing state-of-the-art approaches
model CTR prediction as binary classification problems, where dis-
played events with and without click feedbacks are respectively
considered as positive and negative instances for training and of-
fline validation.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding: Experimental results show significant improvements.

concepts.finding:
To maximize the revenue, the estimation of (2), also referred as
click-through rate (CTR) prediction, is the core problem of build-
ing advertising systems.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
To obtain
a good decision function, many state-of-the-art models for CTR
prediction problems have been proposed (e.g., logistic regression
[3, 21, 25], factorization machines [24], field-aware factorization
machines [13, 14], and deep learning approaches [6, 23, 34]).

concepts.finding:
To further improve
the robustness of our framework, we propose a new way to obtain
the imputation model by taking the advantage of the small but
unbiased set St defined in Section 2.2.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Finally, by connecting the
CTR prediction with recommender systems, we propose an efficient
training algorithm so that the prohibitive O(ˆL) cost can be trickily
avoided if some conditions are satisfied.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Experiments
conducted on real-world data sets show our proposed framework
outperforms not only conventional approaches for CTR prediction
but also some existing counterfactual learning methods.

concepts.finding:
To overcome these difficulties, we propose a novel
framework for counterfactual CTR prediction.

compare our proposed framework against state-of-the-art conven-
tional CTR models and existing counterfactual learning approaches.
